title: rubus × chnoophyllus
intro: Rubus × chnoophyllus, also known as the black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant that combines the characteristics of the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). This plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and ellagic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus × chnoophyllus include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. Rubus × chnoophyllus is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products due to its sweet-tart flavor and high nutritional value.
